{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_df1e6cf845034342a6f2f7aa","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! `xtask corpus` — manage the GRIB2 fixture corpus.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Subcommands:\n4\t//!   list   — print all manifest entries with storage type and local presence\n5\t//!   fetch  — download remote fixtures from B2 (by sha256 or explicit URL)\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Remote-fixture conventions\n8\t//!\n9\t//! Fixtures with `storage=remote` live in `tests/corpus/large/` (gitignored).\n10\t//! The manifest entry must have either:\n11\t//!   - a `url` field with the full download URL, or\n12\t//!   - no `url` field, in which case `GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT` and\n13\t//!     `GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET` env vars are used to build\n14\t//!     `<endpoint>/file/<bucket>/<sha256>`.\n15\t//!\n16\t//! For private buckets set `B2_ACCOUNT_ID` and `B2_APPLICATION_KEY`; for public\n17\t//! buckets no auth is required.\n18\t\n19\tuse std::io::Write as _;\n20\tuse std::path::Path;\n21\t\n22\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n23\t\n24\tuse gribtract_testutil::corpus::{corpus_root, list_fixtures, FixtureEntry};\n25\t\n26\t// ── Public entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n27\t\n28\tpub fn run(rest: &[String]) {\n29\t    let subcommand = rest.first().map(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"\");\n30\t    match subcommand {\n31\t        \"list\" => cmd_list(),\n32\t        \"fetch\" => cmd_fetch(&rest[1..]),\n33\t        \"\" => {\n34\t            eprintln!(\"usage: xtask corpus <list|fetch> [args...]\");\n35\t            eprintln!(\"  list          print all fixtures with storage type and local presence\");\n36\t            eprintln!(\"  fetch         download remote fixtures (all, or --fixture <id>)\");\n37\t            std::process::exit(1);\n38\t        }\n39\t        other => {\n40\t            eprintln!(\"unknown corpus subcommand: {other:?}\");\n41\t            eprintln!(\"available: list, fetch\");\n42\t            std::process::exit(1);\n43\t        }\n44\t    }\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t// ── list ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n48\t\n49\tfn cmd_list() {\n50\t    let fixtures = match list_fixtures() {\n51\t        Ok(f) => f,\n52\t        Err(e) => {\n53\t            eprintln!(\"corpus list: {e}\");\n54\t            std::process::exit(1);\n55\t        }\n56\t    };\n57\t    let root = corpus_root();\n58\t\n59\t    println!(\n60\t        \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  path\",\n61\t        \"id\", \"storage\", \"present\"\n62\t    );\n63\t    println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(80));\n64\t\n65\t    for f in &fixtures {\n66\t        let file_path = root.join(&f.path);\n67\t        let present = if file_path.exists() {\n68\t            \"yes\"\n69\t        } else {\n70\t            \"no\"\n71\t        };\n72\t        println!(\n73\t            \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  {}\",\n74\t            f.id,\n75\t            f.storage,\n76\t            present,\n77\t            file_path.display()\n78\t        );\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    println!();\n82\t    let total = fixtures.len();\n83\t    let remote_count = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").count();\n84\t    let missing_remote = fixtures\n85\t        .iter()\n86\t        .filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\" && !root.join(&f.path).exists())\n87\t        .count();\n88\t    println!(\"{total} total fixtures, {remote_count} remote, {missing_remote} remote missing locally\");\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\t// ── fetch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n92\t\n93\tfn cmd_fetch(args: &[String]) {\n94\t    // Parse optional --fixture <id>\n95\t    let mut specific_id: Option<String> = None;\n96\t    let mut i = 0;\n97\t    while i < args.len() {\n98\t        match args[i].as_str() {\n99\t            \"--fixture\" => {\n100\t                i += 1;\n101\t                specific_id = args.get(i).cloned();\n102\t                if specific_id.is_none() {\n103\t                    eprintln!(\"--fixture requires an argument\");\n104\t                    std::process::exit(1);\n105\t                }\n106\t            }\n107\t            other => {\n108\t                eprintln!(\"unknown flag: {other:?}\");\n109\t                eprintln!(\"usage: xtask corpus fetch [--fixture <id>]\");\n110\t                std::process::exit(1);\n111\t            }\n112\t        }\n113\t        i += 1;\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    let fixtures = match list_fixtures() {\n117\t        Ok(f) => f,\n118\t        Err(e) => {\n119\t            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: cannot load manifest: {e}\");\n120\t            std::process::exit(1);\n121\t        }\n122\t    };\n123\t\n124\t    // Select fixtures to process\n125\t    let targets: Vec<&FixtureEntry> = if let Some(ref id) = specific_id {\n126\t        let matched: Vec<_> = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| &f.id == id).collect();\n127\t        if matched.is_empty() {\n128\t            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: fixture '{id}' not found in manifest\");\n129\t            std::process::exit(1);\n130\t        }\n131\t        matched\n132\t    } else {\n133\t        fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").collect()\n134\t    };\n135\t\n136\t    if targets.is_empty() {\n137\t        println!(\"corpus fetch: no remote fixtures in manifest — nothing to do\");\n138\t        return;\n139\t    }\n140\t\n141\t    let root = corpus_root();\n142\t    let mut fetched = 0usize;\n143\t    let mut already_ok = 0usize;\n144\t    let mut failed = 0usize;\n145\t\n146\t    for entry in &targets {\n147\t        if entry.storage != \"remote\" {\n148\t            // If --fixture was used on a non-remote entry, it's already inline\n149\t            let path = root.join(&entry.path);\n150\t            if path.exists() {\n151\t                println!(\"[ok]      {} (storage={}, already present)\", entry.id, entry.storage);\n152\t                already_ok += 1;\n153\t            } else {\n154\t                eprintln!(\"[missing] {} (storage={}, but file not found at {})\",\n155\t                    entry.id, entry.storage, path.display());\n156\t                failed += 1;\n157\t            }\n158\t            continue;\n159\t        }\n160\t\n161\t        let dest = root.join(&entry.path);\n162\t\n163\t        // Check if already present and valid\n164\t        if dest.exists() {\n165\t            match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n166\t                Ok(true) => {\n167\t                    println!(\"[ok]      {} (already present, sha256 matches)\", entry.id);\n168\t                    already_ok += 1;\n169\t                    continue;\n170\t                }\n171\t                Ok(false) => {\n172\t                    eprintln!(\"[stale]   {} — sha256 mismatch, re-downloading\", entry.id);\n173\t                }\n174\t                Err(e) => {\n175\t                    eprintln!(\"[warn]    {} — cannot read existing file: {e}; re-downloading\", entry.id);\n176\t                }\n177\t            }\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        // Determine download URL\n181\t        let url = match resolve_url(entry) {\n182\t            Ok(u) => u,\n183\t            Err(e) => {\n184\t                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — {e}\", entry.id);\n185\t                failed += 1;\n186\t                continue;\n187\t            }\n188\t        };\n189\t\n190\t        println!(\"[fetch]   {} from {}\", entry.id, url);\n191\t\n192\t        // Create parent directory if needed\n193\t        if let Some(parent) = dest.parent() {\n194\t            if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {\n195\t                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — cannot create directory {}: {e}\", entry.id, parent.display());\n196\t                failed += 1;\n197\t                continue;\n198\t            }\n199\t        }\n200\t\n201\t        // Download\n202\t        match download(&url, &dest) {\n203\t            Ok(bytes_written) => {\n204\t                // Verify sha256\n205\t                match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n206\t                    Ok(true) => {\n207\t                        println!(\n208\t                            \"[done]    {} — {:.1} KB written, sha256 ok\",\n209\t                            entry.id,\n210\t                            bytes_written as f64 / 1024.0\n211\t                        );\n212\t                        fetched += 1;\n213\t                    }\n214\t                    Ok(false) => {\n215\t                        eprintln!(\n216\t                            \"[error]   {} — downloaded {} bytes but sha256 mismatch (expected {})\",\n217\t                            entry.id, bytes_written, entry.sha256\n218\t                        );\n219\t                        // Remove the bad file\n220\t                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n221\t                        failed += 1;\n222\t                    }\n223\t                    Err(e) => {\n224\t                        eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — sha256 verify failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n225\t                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n226\t                        failed += 1;\n227\t                    }\n228\t                }\n229\t            }\n230\t            Err(e) => {\n231\t                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — download failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n232\t                failed += 1;\n233\t            }\n234\t        }\n235\t    }\n236\t\n237\t    println!();\n238\t    println!(\"corpus fetch: {fetched} downloaded, {already_ok} already present, {failed} failed\");\n239\t    if failed > 0 {\n240\t        std::process::exit(1);\n241\t    }\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t// ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n245\t\n246\t/// Resolve the download URL for a remote fixture entry.\n247\t///\n248\t/// Priority:\n249\t/// 1. `url` field in the manifest entry (explicit URL)\n250\t/// 2. expected)\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t/// Minimal base64 encoder (RFC 4648, standard alphabet, with padding).\n324\t/// Used for B2 Basic-auth header — avoids pulling in a base64 crate.\n325\tfn    out.push(TABLE[((n >> 12) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n335\t        if chunk.len() > 1 {\n336\t            out.push(TABLE[((n >> 6) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n337\t        } else {\n338\t `xtask corpus` — manage the GRIB2 fixture corpus.\n//!\n//! Subcommands:\n//!   list   — print all manifest entries with storage type and local presence\n//!   fetch  — download remote fixtures from B2 (by sha256 or explicit URL)\n//!\n//! # Remote-fixture conventions\n//!\n//! Fixtures with `storage=remote` live in `tests/corpus/large/` (gitignored).\n//! The manifest entry must have either:\n//!   - a `url` field with the full download URL, or\n//!   - no `url` field, in which case `GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT` and\n//!     `GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET` env vars are used to build\n//!     `<endpoint>/file/<bucket>/<sha256>`.\n//!\n//! For private buckets set `B2_ACCOUNT_ID` and `B2_APPLICATION_KEY`; for public\n//! buckets no auth is required.\n\nuse std::io::Write as _;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n\nuse gribtract_testutil::corpus::{corpus_root, list_fixtures, FixtureEntry};\n\n// ── Public entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\npub fn run(rest: &[String]) {\n    let subcommand = rest.first().map(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"\");\n    match subcommand {\n        \"list\" => cmd_list(),\n        \"fetch\" => cmd_fetch(&rest[1..]),\n        \"\" => {\n            eprintln!(\"usage: xtask corpus <list|fetch> [args...]\");\n            eprintln!(\"  list          print all fixtures with storage type and local presence\");\n            eprintln!(\"  fetch         download remote fixtures (all, or --fixture <id>)\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n        other => {\n            eprintln!(\"unknown corpus subcommand: {other:?}\");\n            eprintln!(\"available: list, fetch\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── list ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn cmd_list() {\n    let fixtures = match list_fixtures() {\n        Ok(f) => f,\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"corpus list: {e}\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n    let root = corpus_root();\n\n    println!(\n        \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  path\",\n        \"id\", \"storage\", \"present\"\n    );\n    println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(80));\n\n    for f in &fixtures {\n        let file_path = root.join(&f.path);\n        let present = if file_path.exists() {\n            \"yes\"\n        } else {\n            \"no\"\n        };\n        println!(\n            \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  {}\",\n            f.id,\n            f.storage,\n            present,\n            file_path.display()\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!();\n    let total = fixtures.len();\n    let remote_count = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").count();\n    let missing_remote = fixtures\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\" && !root.join(&f.path).exists())\n        .count();\n    println!(\"{total} total fixtures, {remote_count} remote, {missing_remote} remote missing locally\");\n}\n\n// ── fetch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn cmd_fetch(args: &[String]) {\n    // Parse optional --fixture <id>\n    let mut specific_id: Option<String> = None;\n    let mut i = 0;\n    while i < args.len() {\n        match args[i].as_str() {\n            \"--fixture\" => {\n                i += 1;\n                specific_id = args.get(i).cloned();\n                if specific_id.is_none() {\n                    eprintln!(\"--fixture requires an argument\");\n                    std::process::exit(1);\n                }\n            }\n            other => {\n                eprintln!(\"unknown flag: {other:?}\");\n                eprintln!(\"usage: xtask corpus fetch [--fixture <id>]\");\n                std::process::exit(1);\n            }\n        }\n        i += 1;\n    }\n\n    let fixtures = match list_fixtures() {\n        Ok(f) => f,\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: cannot load manifest: {e}\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Select fixtures to process\n    let targets: Vec<&FixtureEntry> = if let Some(ref id) = specific_id {\n        let matched: Vec<_> = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| &f.id == id).collect();\n        if matched.is_empty() {\n            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: fixture '{id}' not found in manifest\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n        matched\n    } else {\n        fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").collect()\n    };\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"corpus fetch: no remote fixtures in manifest — nothing to do\");\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let root = corpus_root();\n    let mut fetched = 0usize;\n    let mut already_ok = 0usize;\n    let mut failed = 0usize;\n\n    for entry in &targets {\n        if entry.storage != \"remote\" {\n            // If --fixture was used on a non-remote entry, it's already inline\n            let path = root.join(&entry.path);\n            if path.exists() {\n                println!(\"[ok]      {} (storage={}, already present)\", entry.id, entry.storage);\n                already_ok += 1;\n            } else {\n                eprintln!(\"[missing] {} (storage={}, but file not found at {})\",\n                    entry.id, entry.storage, path.display());\n                failed += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let dest = root.join(&entry.path);\n\n        // Check if already present and valid\n        if dest.exists() {\n            match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n                Ok(true) => {\n                    println!(\"[ok]      {} (already present, sha256 matches)\", entry.id);\n                    already_ok += 1;\n                    continue;\n                }\n                Ok(false) => {\n                    eprintln!(\"[stale]   {} — sha256 mismatch, re-downloading\", entry.id);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    eprintln!(\"[warn]    {} — cannot read existing file: {e}; re-downloading\", entry.id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Determine download URL\n        let url = match resolve_url(entry) {\n            Ok(u) => u,\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — {e}\", entry.id);\n                failed += 1;\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n\n        println!(\"[fetch]   {} from {}\", entry.id, url);\n\n        // Create parent directory if needed\n        if let Some(parent) = dest.parent() {\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — cannot create directory {}: {e}\", entry.id, parent.display());\n                failed += 1;\n                continue;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Download\n        match download(&url, &dest) {\n            Ok(bytes_written) => {\n                // Verify sha256\n                match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n                    Ok(true) => {\n                        println!(\n                            \"[done]    {} — {:.1} KB written, sha256 ok\",\n                            entry.id,\n                            bytes_written as f64 / 1024.0\n                        );\n                        fetched += 1;\n                    }\n                    Ok(false) => {\n                        eprintln!(\n                            \"[error]   {} — downloaded {} bytes but sha256 mismatch (expected {})\",\n                            entry.id, bytes_written, entry.sha256\n                        );\n                        // Remove the bad file\n                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n                        failed += 1;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — sha256 verify failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n                        failed += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — download failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n                failed += 1;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!();\n    println!(\"corpus fetch: {fetched} downloaded, {already_ok} already present, {failed} failed\");\n    if failed > 0 {\n        std::process::exit(1);\n    }\n}\n\n// ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Resolve the download URL for a remote fixture entry.\n///\n/// Priority:\n/// 1. `url` field in the manifest entry (explicit URL)\n/// 2. Env vars `GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT` + `GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET` + sha256 key\n///    → `<endpoint>/file/<bucket>/<sha256>`\nfn resolve_url(entry: &FixtureEntry) -> Result<String, String> {\n    if let Some(ref url) = entry.url {\n        return Ok(url.clone());\n    }\n\n    let endpoint = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\").map_err(|_| {\n        \"no download URL in manifest entry and GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is not set. \\\n         Set GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT (e.g. https://f000.backblazeb2.com) and \\\n         GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET, or add a 'url' field to the manifest entry\"\n            .to_string()\n    })?;\n    let bucket = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\").map_err(|_| {\n        \"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is set but GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET is not\".to_string()\n    })?;\n\n    let endpoint = endpoint.trim_end_matches('/');\n    Ok(format!(\"{}/file/{}/{}\", endpoint, bucket, entry.sha256))\n}\n\n/// Download URL to a file, returning the number of bytes written.\nfn download(url: &str, dest: &Path) -> Result<u64, String> {\n    // Build request; attach auth if env vars are set\n    let mut req = ureq::get(url);\n\n    if let (Ok(account_id), Ok(app_key)) = (\n        std::env::var(\"B2_ACCOUNT_ID\"),\n        std::env::var(\"B2_APPLICATION_KEY\"),\n    ) {\n        let creds = format!(\"{}:{}\", account_id, app_key);\n        // Base64 encode credentials\n        let b64 = base64_encode(creds.as_bytes());\n        req = req.set(\"Authorization\", &format!(\"Basic {b64}\"));\n    }\n\n    let resp = req.call().map_err(|e| format!(\"HTTP GET {url}: {e}\"))?;\n    if resp.status() != 200 {\n        return Err(format!(\n            \"HTTP {} from {url}\",\n            resp.status()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let mut reader = resp.into_reader();\n    let mut file = std::fs::File::create(dest)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot create {}: {e}\", dest.display()))?;\n\n    let mut buf = [0u8; 65536];\n    let mut total = 0u64;\n    loop {\n        let n = std::io::Read::read(&mut reader, &mut buf)\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"read error: {e}\"))?;\n        if n == 0 {\n            break;\n        }\n        file.write_all(&buf[..n])\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"write error: {e}\"))?;\n        total += n as u64;\n    }\n\n    Ok(total)\n}\n\n/// Verify a file's SHA-256. Returns `Ok(true)` if it matches, `Ok(false)` if not,\n/// `Err` if the file cannot be read.\nfn verify_sha256(path: &Path, expected: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {\n    let bytes =\n        std::fs::read(path).map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read {}: {e}\", path.display()))?;\n    let digest = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(&bytes));\n    Ok(digest == expected)\n}\n\n/// Minimal base64 encoder (RFC 4648, standard alphabet, with padding).\n/// Used for B2 Basic-auth header — avoids pulling in a base64 crate.\nfn base64_encode(input: &[u8]) -> String {\n    const TABLE: &[u8] = b\"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/\";\n    let mut out = String::with_capacity(input.len().div_ceil(3) * 4);\n    for chunk in input.chunks(3) {\n        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;\n        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let n = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;\n        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 18) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 12) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        if chunk.len() > 1 {\n            out.push(TABLE[((n >> 6) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        } else {\n            out.push('=');\n        }\n        if chunk.len() > 2 {\n            out.push(TABLE[(n & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        } else {\n            out.push('=');\n        }\n    }\n    out\n}\n\n// ── tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn base64_encode_roundtrip() {\n        // RFC 4648 test vectors\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"\"), \"\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"f\"), \"Zg==\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fo\"), \"Zm8=\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foo\"), \"Zm9v\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foob\"), \"Zm9vYg==\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fooba\"), \"Zm9vYmE=\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foobar\"), \"Zm9vYmFy\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn resolve_url_uses_explicit_url() {\n        let entry = \"remote\".into(),\n            url: Some(\"https://example.com/test.grib2\".into()),\n        };\n        let url = resolve_url(&entry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(url, \"https://example.com/test.grib2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn resolve_url_fails_without_env_or_url() {\n        // Remove env vars if set\n      std::env::set_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\", \"gribtract-corpus\");\n        let entry = FixtureEntry {\n            id: \"test\".into(),\n            path: \"large/test.grib2\".into(),\n Reads `tests/corpus/manifest.json` in the workspace root, verifies each\n4\t//! fixture's SHA-256 on first load, and returns the raw bytes.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! # Finding the workspace root\n7\t//!\n8\t//! `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` for this crate is `<workspace>/crates/gribtract-testutil`.\n9\t//! We walk two levels up to find the workspace root. This is fragile only if the\n10\t//! crate moves within the workspace; callers can override with `GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT`.\n11\t\n12\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n13\tuse serde::Deserialize;\n14\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n15\t\n16\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n17\tstruct Manifest {\n18\t    version: u32,\n19\t    fixtures: Vec<FixtureEntry>,\n20\t}\n21\t\n22\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]\n23\tpub struct FixtureEntry {\n24\t    pub id: String,\n25\t    pub path: String,\n26\t    pub sha256: String,\n27\t    pub size_bytes: u64,\n28\t    pub storage: String,\n29\t    /// Optional explicit download URL for storage=remote fixtures.\n30\t    /// If absent, the fetch command derives the URL from env vars\n31\t    /// GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT and GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET using the sha256 as the key.\n32\t    #[serde(default)]\n33\t    pub url: Option<String>,\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t/// Locate the `tests/corpus/` directory for this workspace.\n37\t///\n38\t/// Checks `GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT` env var first; falls back to\n39\t/// `<CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR>/../../tests/corpus`.\n40\tpub fn corpus_root() -> PathBuf {\n41\t    if let Ok(root) = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT\") {\n42\t        return PathBuf::from(root);\n43\t    }\n44\t    let manifest_dir = env!(\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\");\n45\t    Path::new(manifest_dir)\n46\t        .join(\"../..\")\n47\t        .join(\"tests/corpus\")\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t/// Load and parse the fixture manifest.\n51\tfn load_manifest() -> Result<Manifest, String> {\n52\t    let manifest_path = corpus_root().join(\"manifest.json\");\n53\t    let json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path)\n54\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read manifest {}: {}\", manifest_path.display(), e))?;\n55\t    let manifest: Manifest = serde_json::from_str(&json)\n56\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot parse manifest: {}\", e))?;\n57\t    if manifest.version != 1 {\n58\t        return Err(format!(\"unsupported manifest version: {}\", manifest.version));\n59\t    }\n60\t    Ok(manifest)\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\t/// Return all entries from the manifest.\n64\tpub fn list_fixtures() -> Result<Vec<FixtureEntry>, String> {\n65\t    Ok(load_manifest()?.fixtures)\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t/// Find the manifest entry for a fixture by id.\n69\tpub fn fixture_entry(id: &str) -> Result<FixtureEntry, String> {\n70\t    let manifest = load_manifest()?;\n71\t    manifest\n72\t        .fixtures\n73\t        .into_iter()\n74\t        .find(|f| f.id == id)\n75\t        .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"fixture '{}' not found in manifest\", id))\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t/// Load fixture bytes by id, verifying SHA-256.\n79\t///\n80\t/// Returns `Err` if the fixture is not in the manifest, the file is missing,\n81\t/// or the digest does not match.\n82\tpub fn load(id: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>, String> {\n83\t    let entry = fixture_entry(id)?;\n84\t\n85\t    if entry.storage == \"remote\" {\n86\t        return Err(format!(\n87\t            \"fixture '{}' has storage=remote — run `cargo xtask corpus fetch {}` first\",\n88\t            id, id\n89\t        ));\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    let file_path = corpus_root().join(&entry.path);\n93\t    let bytes = std::fs::read(&file_path)\n94\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read fixture {}: {}\", file_path.display(), Corpus fixture loader.\n//!\n//! Reads `tests/corpus/manifest.json` in the workspace root, verifies each\n//! fixture's SHA-256 on first load, and returns the raw bytes.\n//!\n//! # Finding the workspace root\n//!\n//! `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` for this crate is `<workspace>/crates/gribtract-testutil`.\n//! We walk two levels up to find the workspace root. This is fragile only if the\n//! crate moves within the workspace; callers can override with `GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct Manifest {\n    version: u32,\n    fixtures: Vec<FixtureEntry>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]\npub struct FixtureEntry {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub path: String,\n    pub sha256: String,\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n    pub storage: String,\n    /// Optional explicit download URL for storage=remote fixtures.\n    /// If absent, the fetch command derives the URL from env vars\n    /// GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT and GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET using the sha256 as the key.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub url: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Locate the `tests/corpus/` directory for this workspace.\n///\n/// Checks `GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT` env var first; falls back to\n/// `<CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR>/../../tests/corpus`.\npub fn corpus_root() -> PathBuf {\n    if let Ok(root) = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_CORPUS_ROOT\") {\n        return PathBuf::from(root);\n    }\n    let manifest_dir = env!(\"CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR\");\n    Path::new(manifest_dir)\n        .join(\"../..\")\n        .join(\"tests/corpus\")\n}\n\n/// Load and parse the fixture manifest.\nfn load_manifest() -> Result<Manifest, String> {\n    let manifest_path = corpus_root().join(\"manifest.json\");\n    let json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read manifest {}: {}\", manifest_path.display(), e))?;\n    let manifest: Manifest = serde_json::from_str(&json)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot parse manifest: {}\", e))?;\n    if manifest.version != 1 {\n        return Err(format!(\"unsupported manifest version: {}\", manifest.version));\n    }\n    Ok(manifest)\n}\n\n/// Return all entries from the manifest.\npub fn list_fixtures() -> Result<Vec<FixtureEntry>, String> {\n    Ok(load_manifest()?.fixtures)\n}\n\n/// Find the manifest entry for a fixture by id.\npub fn fixture_entry(id: &str) -> Result<FixtureEntry, String> {\n    let manifest = load_manifest()?;\n    manifest\n        .fixtures\n        .into_iter()\n        .find(|f| f.id == id)\n        .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"fixture '{}' not found in manifest\", id))\n}\n\n/// Load fixture bytes by id, verifying SHA-256.\n///\n/// Returns `Err` if the fixture is not in the manifest, the file is missing,\n/// or the digest does not match.\npub fn load(id: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>, String> {\n    let entry = fixture_entry(id)?;\n\n    if entry.storage == \"remote\" {\n        return Err(format!(\n            \"fixture '{}' has storage=remote — run `cargo xtask corpus fetch {}` first\",\n            id, id\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let file_path = corpus_root().join(&entry.path);\n    let bytes = std::fs::read(&file_path)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read fixture {}: {}\", file_path.display(), e))?;\n\n    // Verify size\n    if bytes.len() as u64 != entry.size_bytes {\n        return Err(format!(\n            \"fixture '{}' size mismatch: manifest says {} bytes, file is {}\",\n            id, entry.size_bytes, bytes.len()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Verify SHA-256\n    let digest = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(&bytes));\n    if digest != entry.sha256 {\n        return Err(format!(\n            \"fixture '{}' SHA-256 mismatch:\\n  expected {}\\n  got      {}\",\n            id, entry.sha256, digest\n        ));\n    }\n\n    Ok(bytes)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn manifest_parses() {\n        let manifest = load_manifest().expect(\"manifest should parse\");\n        assert_eq!(manifest.version, 1);\n        assert!(!manifest.fixtures.is_empty(), \"manifest should have at least one fixture\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn gfs_anl_t2m_5x5_loads_and_verifies() {\n        let bytes = load(\"gfs_anl_t2m_5x5\").expect(\"fixture should load\");\n\n        // GRIB2 magic at start\n        assert_eq!(&bytes[..4], b\"GRIB\", \"missing GRIB magic\");\n\n        // Edition 2 at byte 7\n        assert_eq!(bytes[7], 2, \"expected GRIB edition 2\");\n\n        // Total length encoded in bytes 8-15 (big-endian u64)\n        let msg_len = u64::from_be_bytes(bytes[8..16].try_into().unwrap());\n        assert_eq!(msg_len as usize, bytes.len(), \"encoded length should match file size\");\n\n        // End marker \"7777\"\n        assert_eq!(&bytes[bytes.len() - 4..], b\"7777\", \"missing 7777 end marker\");\n\n        // Discipline = 0 (meteorological products)\n        assert_eq!(bytes[6], 0, \"discipline should be 0 (meteorological)\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn missing_fixture_returns_err() {\n        assert!(load(\"does_not_exist\").is_err());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":153,"startLine":1,"totalLines":153}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_start","index":9,"content_block":{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"call_2d4cd78127d841f486bc020c","content":"[{\"title\": \"NOAA Global Forecast System (GFS)\", \"url\": \"https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-gfs-bdp-pds/\", \"content\": \"### Description\\n\\nNOTE - Upgrade NCEP Global Forecast System to v16.3.0 - Effective November 29, 2022 See notification HERE\\n\\nThe Global Forecast System (GFS) is a weather forecast model produced\\nby the National Centers for Environmental Prediction (NCEP). Dozens of\\natmospheric and land-soil variables are available through this dataset,\\nfrom temperatures, winds, and precipitation to soil moisture and\\natmospheric ozone concentration. The entire globe is covered by the GFS\\nat a base horizontal resolution of 18 miles (28 kilometers) between grid\\npoints, which is used by the operational forecasters who predict weather\\nout to 16 days in the future. Horizontal resolution drops to 44 miles\\n(70 kilometers) between grid point for forecasts between one week and two\\nweeks.\\n\\nThe NOAA Global Forecast Systems (GFS) Warm Start Initial Conditions are\\nproduced by the National Centers for Environmental Prediction Center (NCEP)\\nto run operational deterministic medium-range numerical weather predictions.  \\nThe GFS is built with the GFDL Finite-Volume Cubed-Sphere Dynamical Core (FV3)\\nand the Grid-Point Statistical Interpolation (GSI) data assimilation system.  \\nPlease visit the links below in the Documentation section to find more details\\nabout the model and the data assimilation systems. The current operational\\nGFS is run at 64 layers in the vertical extending from the surface to the upper\\nstratosphere and on six cubic-sphere tiles at the C768 or 13-km horizontal\\nresolution. A new version of the GFS that has 127 layers extending to the\\nmesopause will be implemented for operation on February 3, 2021. These initial\\nconditions are made available four times per day for running forecasts at the\\n00Z, 06Z, 12Z and 18Z cycles, respectively. For each cycle, the dataset\\ncontains the first guess of the atmosphere states found in the directory\\n./gdas.yyyymmdd/hh-6/RESTART, which are 6-hour GDAS forecast from the last\\ncycle, and atmospheric analysis increments and surface analysis for the current\\ncycle found in the directory ./gfs.yyyymmdd/hh, which are produced by the data\\nassimilation systems.\\n\\n#### Update Frequency\\n\\n4 times a day, every 6 hours starting at midnight UTC\\n\\n#### License\\n\\nNOAA data disseminated through NODD are open to the public and can be used as desired.\\n\\nNOAA makes data openly available to ensure maximum use of our data, and to spur and encourage exploration and innovation throughout the industry. NOAA requests attribution for the use or dissemination of unaltered NOAA data. However, it is not permissible to state or imply endorsement by or affiliation with NOAA. If you modify NOAA data, you may not state or imply that it is original, unaltered NOAA data.\\n\\n#### Documentation\\n\\nhttps://www.emc.ncep.noaa.gov/emc/pages/numerical_forecast_systems/gfs.php\\n\\n#### Managed By\\n\\n![Image 1: NOAA](https://registry.opendata.aws/img/logos/noaa-logo.png)\\n\\nSee all datasets managed by NOAA.\\n\\n#### Contact\\n\\nFor questions regarding data content or quality, visit the NOAA GFS site. Maddy\\n- Validation and Application of the Accu-Waves Operational Platform for Wave Forecasts at Ports by Christos Makris et al.\", \"metadata\": {\"viewport\": \"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\", \"lang\": \"en\"}, \"external\": {\"stylesheet\": {\"/css/main.css\": {}, \"https://assets.opendata.aws/css/bootstrap/3.4.1/bootstrap.min.css\": {}}, \"shortcut\": {\"https://a0.awsstatic.com/libra-css/images/site/fav/favicon.ico\": {\"type\": \"image/ico\"}}, \"alternate\": {\"/rss.xml\": {\"type\": \"application/rss+xml\"}}, \"apple-touch-icon\": {\"https://a0.awsstatic.com/libra-css/images/site/touch-icon-ipad-144-smile.png\": {\"sizes\": \"144x144\"}, \"https://a0.awsstatic.com/libra-css/images/site/touch-icon-iphone-114-smile.png\": {\"sizes\": \"114x114\"}}}}]"}},"session_id":"9a3c269f-59df-476d-a272-e4554665ac7e","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"4d64ee11-cab3-4c8d-b29f-da07951d890b"}
